preamble: The phonetic transformation from the voiced alveolar affricate /dz/ to the voiceless palatal plosive /c/ is a documented historical shift involving multiple phonetic changes. This shift typically occurs in specific phonological environments, such as at the end of words or syllables, or when preceding voiceless consonants, as part of a broader process of devoicing and assimilation.
phoneticEffects: Devoicing (voiced to voiceless), Palatalization (alveolar to palatal), and Deaffrication (affricate to plosive)

examples.note: Voiced consonants, including /dz/, generally become unvoiced /c/ at the end of words and syllables or when preceding unvoiced consonants.
